dailyJudgementSnippet: Anthropic dominated the day through the huge Claude Sonnet 4.6 thread, where commenters saw real model progress and kept treating Claude as a leading benchmark even while pressing hard on safety numbers and obvious failure cases; smaller threads still praised Claude and Claude Code for practical coding work [E1][E3][E4].
winnerExplanation: Anthropic had the broadest and strongest direct HN signal: a massive front-page Claude launch with extensive debate about capability versus safety [E1], a separate prompt-injection discussion centered on Claude Opus [E2], and additional concrete praise for Claude in reverse-engineering and hobby coding workflows [E3][E4]. That combination gave Anthropic the best overall day once both sentiment and coverage were weighed together.

anthropic.judgementSnippet: Claude Sonnet 4.6 was the main driver: commenters called progress fast and benchmarked Anthropic models highly, but also highlighted uncomfortable safety numbers and simple failure cases; elsewhere, Claude and Claude Code still got practical praise for reverse-engineering and personal coding work [E1][E2][E3][E4].

google_gemini.judgementSnippet: The only meaningful Gemini signal was as a comparison point: a commenter described a recent Anthropic jump as the biggest since Gemini 2.5 Pro, using Gemini as a benchmark for notable progress [E5].

microsoft_copilot.judgementSnippet: In the Gentoo migration discussion, commenters specifically pointed to Copilot and GitHub's broader AI push as reasons developers may want off GitHub, tying Microsoft Copilot to ecosystem backlash rather than product pull [E6].

evidence.summary: The Claude Sonnet 4.6 launch dominated HN; commenters called the pace of improvement remarkable and compared Sonnet favorably to earlier Anthropic models, but others focused on concerning prompt-injection numbers in Anthropic's own safety evals and on simple reasoning failures.

HackMyClaw explicitly used Claude Opus as a prompt-injection target; discussion centered on how realistic the setup was and whether apparent resistance reflected model robustness or test design, leaving a mixed signal on Anthropic's safety posture.

In a reverse-engineering thread, a commenter said Claude was pretty good at turning assembly into readable Objective-C, giving Anthropic a concrete product-quality endorsement outside the launch thread.
A side-project thread included a direct endorsement of ClaudeCode as a game changer for personal software projects, adding a smaller but clearly positive workflow signal for Anthropic.
Within the Claude Sonnet 4.6 discussion, one commenter described a recent Anthropic advance as the biggest leap since Gemini 2.5 Pro, implicitly treating Gemini as a recent high benchmark.

In the Gentoo-on-Codeberg thread, commenters said Copilot and GitHub's AI push were part of why developers are considering leaving GitHub, making Microsoft Copilot a negative symbol of platform overreach.
